title: Supply and the law of supply
description: Practice what you've learned about supply and the law of supply in this exercise.
topic_path: Supply and the law of supply

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following statements *correctly* describes how an event will impact supply or quantity supplied?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: An increase in costs of production causes the supply curve to increase.
An increase in demand causes an increase in supply.
A price increase causes an increase in supply.
A price decrease increases quantity supplied.
A decrease in resource costs causes an increase in the supply curve

assessment_items.question_content:
This graph illustrates two supply curves.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Which of the following movements would be described as an increase in the supply of a good?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: The figure presents a graph of two supply curves in the first quadrant of the cartesian plane. The horizontal axis is labeled Q and the vertical axis is labeled P. One curve is labeled S 1 and starts at the vertical axis above the origin at coordinates 0 and 3 and moves up and to the right and ends at coordinates 8 and 7. The second curve is labeled S 2 and is directly above and parallel to the first curve and starts at coordinates 0 and 5 and moves up and to the right and ends at coordinates 8 and 9. There are four points in the graph. The first point is on the first curve at coordinates 2 and 4 and is labeled B. The second point is on the first curve at coordinates 6 and 6 and is labeled D. The third point is on the second curve at coordinates 2 and 6 and is labeled A. The fourth point is on the second curve at coordinates 6 and 8 and is labeled C.
options.caption: Two supply curves

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following best describes the law of supply?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: As price increases, quantity supplied increases.
Producers sell the same amount of a good no matter its price.
Sellers set the price that demanders pay.

assessment_items.question_content:
**According to the law of supply, what happens when the price of a good increases?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: People are willing to buy what is supplied.
The supply curve shifts to the left.
The supply curve shifts to the right.

assessment_items.question_content:
The table below shows the supply schedules for 3 sellers in a market. 

Price | Seller 1’s quantity supplied | Seller 2’s quantity supplied | Seller 3’s quantity supplied
-|-|-|-
$\$1$ | $0$ | $0$ | $1$
$\$2$ | $1$ | $0$ | $2$
$\$3$ | $3$ | $1$ | $3$
$\$4$ | $5$ | $2$ | $4$
$\$5$ | $7$ | $3$ | $5$

**How much is supplied in this market at a price of $\$2$ and at a price of $\$5$?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
The market supply at each price is the horizontal sum of the quantities supplied at each price. 
The market supply at \$2 is 3:

$\begin{align}\text{Market supply at }\$2&= 1+0+2\\\\
&= 3\end{align}$
The market supply at $\$5$ is $15$:

$\begin{align}\text{Market supply at } \$2&= 1+0+2\\\\
&= 3\\\\
\text{Market supply at }\$5&=7+3+5\\\\
&=15\end{align}$

choices.content: $3$ units when the price is $\$2$; $15$ units when the price is $\$5$
$2$ units when the price is $\$2$; $5$ units when the price is $\$5$
$3$ units when the price is $\$2$; $7$ units when the price is $\$5$
$2$ units when the price is $\$2$; $8$ units when the price is $\$5$
$0$ units when the price is $\$2$; $3$ units when the price is $\$5$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following describes the movement indicated by the arrow in the graph shown?**

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ radio 2]]

options.alt: The figure presents a graph of two supply curves in the first quadrant of the cartesian plane. The horizontal axis is labeled Q and the vertical axis is labeled P. One curve is labeled S 1 and starts at the vertical axis above the origin at coordinates 0 and 5 and moves up and to the right and ends at coordinates 7 and 10. The second curve is labeled S 2 and is directly below and parallel to the first curve and starts at coordinates 0 and 2 and moves up and to the right and ends at coordinates 7 and 7. There is an arrow pointing to the right from the first curve to the second curve.
options.caption: Two supply curves

choices.content: An increase in the quantity supplied

assessment_items.question_content:
Use the figure shown to answer the question.

[[☃ image 1]]

**According to each supply curve, what quantities would be produced at $\$30$?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: The figure presents a graph of two supply curves in the first quadrant of the cartesian plane. The horizontal axis is labeled Q and the vertical axis is labeled P. One curve is labeled S 1 and starts at the vertical axis above the origin at coordinates 0 and 10 and moves up and to the right and ends at coordinates 80 and 50. The second curve is labeled S 2 and is directly above and parallel to the first curve and starts at coordinates 0 and 30 and moves up and to the right and ends at coordinates 80 and 70.
options.caption: Two supply curves
